original: Indian "human computer"Shakuntala Devisets a world record by mentally multiplying two random 13-digit numbers in 28 seconds; She correctly answered that 7,686,369,774,870 × 2,465,099,745,779 = 18,947,668,177,995,426,462,773,730 !
Space shuttle Challenger 2 launches the first American woman into space, astronautSally Ride
World record for greatest duration for a single lighting flash of 17.1 seconds during thunderstorm over Uruguay and Argentina according to World Meteorological Organization
Five monks at Canterbury report something exploding on the moon shortly after sunset (only known observation)
Chris Van Allsburg, American author and illustrator of children's books (Jumanji, Polar Express), born in Grand Rapids, Michigan[1][2]
David Tennant's first appearance as the Tenth Doctor in BBC "Doctor Who" episode "The Parting of the Ways"
American aviatorAmelia Earhartbecomes the first woman to fly across the Atlantic Ocean (as a passenger), landing at Burry Port, Wales
Soyuz capsule returns to Earth 1st British International Space Station astronaut Tim Peake, Russian Yuri Malenchenko and American Timothy Kopra after 186 days
American photographer Thomas Martin Easterly takes the earliest known photograph of lightning using the daguerreotype process in St. Louis, Missouri
All BlackJonah Lomuscores the try of the Rugby World Cup, running over Mike Catt in New Zealand's 45-29 defeat of England
W. H. Richardson, an African-American inventor, patents the children's carriage (Patent no. 405599)
Chennai, India's 6th largest city with 4 million people, runs out of water as its reservoirs dry up
Rare magnitude-four earthquake causes a tsunami to hit Nuugaatsiaq in northwestern Greenland
Detroit Tigers trail Chicago White Sox, 13-1 at Bennett Park, Detroit; recover to win, 16-15 for the biggest comeback in Major League Baseball history
GeneralCharles de Gaullemakes his first speech on the BBC to the French people, since arriving in London, an appeal to defy Nazi occupiers - regarded as the beginning of French Resistance during WWII
Battle of Waterloo:Napoleon Bonaparteand France are defeated by British forces under the Duke of Wellington and Prussian troops under Field Marshal von Blücher
Gene Roddenberrysurvives plane crash in the Syrian Desert while working for Pan American World Airways
Aleksander Popov swims world record 100m free style (48.21 sec)
Space Shuttle test model "Enterprise" carries a crew aloft for 1st time, it was fixed to a modified Boeing 747
The first Kazakh space satellite, KazSat is launched
Rema’s "Calm Down", already the most successful African song, becomes the first Afrobeats single to earn over 1 billion on-demand streams in the US
Jimi Hendrixand Pete Townsend wage a battle of guitars at the Monetery Pop Festival
Closing day of the Monterey International Pop Festival, Southern California, featuring first major US appearances ofJimi Hendrix,Janis Joplin, The Who, andOtis Redding
Bernard W. Robinson, becomes 1st African American ensign in US Navy
Flooding in Bangladesh and nearby Indian states leave four million stranded and without electricity, with at least 41 dead as monsoon floods become more frequent and extreme
Pope Francisblames human selfishness for global warming in his encyclical, named "Laudato Si (Be Praised), On the Care of Our Common Home"
USPresident Trumporders US military to set up sixth branch of the military - a space force
Susan B. Anthonyis fined $100 ($2,200 in 2020 value) for voting for US President in Rochester, New York; she refuses to pay, and no further action against her is taken
English QuakerWilliam Pennfounds Philadelphia, in the Pennsylvania Colony
Winston Churchillgives his "This was their finest hour" speech to the House of Commons, urging perseverance in the war after the Dunkirk evacuation and the fall of France
"The Fast and the Furious" film directed by Rob Cohen starringPaul Walker,Vin Dieseland Michelle Rodriguez premieres
Heike Friedrich swims female world record 200m freestyle (1:57.55)
England cricket captain Eoin Morgan smashes a ODI world record 17 sixes against Afghanistan in his side's 150-run World Cup victory at Old Trafford; Morgan, 148 from 71 balls
Pakistan defeat India to win cricket's Champions Trophy at the Oval in London by 180 runs, Fakhar Zaman scores 114 runs
ICC Men's Cricket Champions Trophy, The Oval, London: Pakistan wins the competition for the first time with a 180-run victory over India; Player of the Series: Hasan Ali (PAK) 13 wickets
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Råsunda Stadium, Stockholm, Sweden: Hege Riise & Marianne Pettersen score within 3 minutes of each other to give Norway a 2-0 win over Germany
Cleveland Indians score an American League record 14 runs in 1st inning; beat Philadelphia A's, 21-2 at Cleveland Stadium
Red Sox rookie Eugene Stephens is 1st player in AL history to register 3 hits in an inning as Boston scores 17 runs in the 7th during 23-3 rout of visiting Detroit Tigers at Fenway Park, Boston
Yankee pitchers pick-off 3 Toronto Blue Jays
MLB St Louis CardinalsLou Brock& Hector Cruz hit inside-the-park HRs
In his 18th world heavyweight title defenceJoe LouisKOsBilly Connin 13th round in front of a crowd of 54,487 at the Polo Grounds, NYC

President Jimmy Carter hosts the first White House Jazz Festival; performers include Lionel Hampton, Teddy Wilson, Ornette Coleman, Dizzy Gillespie, Max Roach, Cecil Taylor, Zoot Sims, Herbie Hancock, Dexter Gordon, Sonny Rollins, Stan Getz, Chick Corea, George Benson, McCoy Tyner, and Ron Carter
Martin St. Louis, Canadian Hockey HOF right wing (Stanley Cup 2004 TB Lightning; World Cup of Hockey gold 2004) and coach (Montreal Canadiens), born in Laval, Quebec

Bruce Smith, American College-Pro Football HOF defensive end (Virginia Tech; NFL Defensive Player of the Year 1990, 96; 8×First-team All-Pro; 11×Pro Bowl; Buffalo Bills, Washington Redskins), born in Norfolk, Virginia
Delia Smith, English cook and television presenter, born in Woking, England
Du Yun, Chinese-American composer (Angel's Bone - Pulitzer Prize for Music, 2017), born in Shanghai, China
Nigel Owens, Welsh rugby union referee (record holder for most Test matches refereed 100), born in Mynyddcerrig, Wales
Ella Chen, Taiwanese actress, singer and member of the girl-group S.H.E, born in Pingtung, Taiwan
Jimmie Allen, American country music singer-songwriter ("Best Shot", "Make Me Want To"), born in Milton, Delaware
Evan Mobley, American NBA player (Cleveland Cavaliers), born in San Diego, California
Sandy Alomar Jr., Puerto Rican baseball catcher (6 × MLB All-Star; AL Rookie of the Year 1990 Cleveland Indians), born in Salinas, Puerto Rico
Pål Pot Pamparius [Pål Bøttger Kjærnes], Norwegian rock guitarist and piano player (Turbonegro), born in Kolbotn, Norway
Jeff Saturday, American football center (6 × Pro Bowl; First-team All-Pro 2005, 07; Super Bowl 2006; Indianapolis Colts) and sports analyst (ESPN), born in Atlanta, Georgia
Tara Platt, American actress and voice actress for Japanese anime films (Temari-Naruto), born in Fairfax, Virginia
Noor Sabri, Iraqi footballer and goalkeeper (100 caps, winner Asian Cup 2007), born in Baghdad, Iraq[1]
Comet Churyunov-Gerasimenko at perihelion
Vaccine to prevent hoof & mouth disease announced
US President Carter and Soviet leaderLeonid Brezhnevsign SALT II treaty limiting nuclear weapons
Voting Rights Act of 1965 extended by US Senate by 85-8 vote
Brothers Edward and Robert Jordan plant 1st Macadamia nuts in Oahu, Hawaii
